title: iris unguicularis subsp. syriaca
intro: Iris unguicularis subsp. syriaca, also known as the Syrian or Syrian Iris, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the iris family. It is native to the Middle East and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and fight off infections. The herbal preparations of Iris unguicularis subsp. syriaca include dried roots and rhizomes, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
